title: Gambling
subtitle: An Abomination of Satan That Sows Enmity and Ruins Lives

verses.arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّمَا الْخَمْرُ وَالْمَيْسِرُ وَالْأَنصَابُ وَالْأَزْلَامُ رِجْسٌ مِّنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ فَاجْتَنِبُوهُ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
verses.translation: O you who have believed, indeed, intoxicants, gambling, [sacrificing on] stone altars, and divining arrows are but defilement from the work of Satan, so avoid it that you may be successful.

verses.arabic: إِنَّمَا يُرِيدُ الشَّيْطَانُ أَن يُوقِعَ بَيْنَكُمُ الْعَدَاوَةَ وَالْبَغْضَاءَ فِي الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ وَيَصُدَّكُمْ عَن ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ وَعَنِ الصَّلَاةِ ۖ فَهَلْ أَنتُم مُّنتَهُونَ
verses.translation: Satan only wants to cause between you animosity and hatred through intoxicants and gambling and to avert you from the remembrance of Allah and from prayer. So will you not desist?

verses.arabic: يَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ ۖ قُلْ فِيهِمَا إِثْمٌ كَبِيرٌ وَمَنَافِعُ لِلنَّاسِ وَإِثْمُهُمَا أَكْبَرُ مِن نَّفْعِهِمَا
verses.translation: They ask you about wine and gambling. Say, 'In them is great sin and [yet, some] benefit for people. But their sin is greater than their benefit.'

brief_explanation: The Quran places gambling (maysir) in the same sentence as wine, idolatry, and divination — all classified as 'rijs min amal al-shaytan' (defilement from the work of Satan). Allah explicitly states that Satan's goal through gambling is to plant enmity and hatred between people and to distract them from the dhikr of Allah and from prayer — pointing to its deep social and spiritual destructiveness. Gambling devours wealth through falsehood, creates addiction, destroys families, and builds a culture of greed and rivalry. The Quran's first response when asked about gambling was to call it 'ithm kabir' (great sin), even before the final outright prohibition was revealed.
capital_punishment: There is no hadd (fixed corporal punishment) specifically prescribed for gambling. It is a major sin requiring cessation and repentance. The Islamic state may impose ta'zir (fines, imprisonment, public censure) for those who operate or frequent gambling establishments. Winnings from gambling are considered unlawfully acquired wealth (mal haram) and must be disposed of in charity — they cannot be kept or returned as a reward. The state has authority to close gambling venues and prohibit their operation under the principle of preventing corruption (sadd al-dhara'i).
